nodes.grounding_span: I also use it for tracking meeting notes and tasks

nodes.grounding_span: Leaving tasks unassigned when they were assigned

nodes.grounding_span: Some tasks are delegating to the client and not me, and those would be nice as a reminder

nodes.grounding_span: I tend to ask what I need and then take it from there

nodes.grounding_span: I do think my company (being a software company) will get involved and maybe even enlist it's uses in our software

nodes.label: Efficiency and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: it could expedite whole parts of our jobs

nodes.label: Accuracy and reliability
nodes.grounding_span: I do review the overview at the end to ensure those notes are accurate

nodes.label: Skill maintenance and self-reliance
nodes.grounding_span: making sure I can operate without any AI help if needed

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for mundane tasks
Optimism about future AI integration
nodes.grounding_span: I could see it quickly becoming something that could expedite whole parts of our jobs

nodes.label: Caution about AI accuracy in task assignment
nodes.grounding_span: Leaving tasks unassigned when they were assigned

nodes.label: Preference for AI in client-facing reminders only
nodes.grounding_span: Some tasks are delegating to the client and not me, and those would be nice as a reminder for the AI to handle. On tasks for myself, or internal tasks my team needs to do, I don't need the reminder

nodes.label: Pragmatic task decomposition — uses AI for specific sub-tasks rather than full delegation
nodes.grounding_span: I tend to ask what I need and then take it from there

nodes.label: Proactive skill preservation — actively trains to maintain ability without AI
nodes.grounding_span: I actively train on new things being changed in our software, and making sure I can operate without any AI help if needed
